MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laura Berger in Propria Persona.

Ms. Berger stated that she filed the petition with waivers, that she did not know she had to provide notice and that she understands that she needs to provide an order. The Court stated that there are two issues that need to be addressed, that one is providing notice and that the other is filing a proof of service. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account is continued to August 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Continued To Apr-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laura Berger in Propria Persona.

Ms. Berger stated that she filed the petition for final distribution and that it is pending. Status on close of estate is continued to April 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laura Berger in Propria Persona.

Ms. Berger stated that she is almost ready to distribute the estate, that there was a large amount of funds in pay on death accounts, that estate taxes were required and are done, and that she wants to be certain that the tax amounts are correct. Ms. Berger also spoke of a painting that is to be split between herself and her two Sisters, and of how distribution is going to be made (30% to each of the three Sisters and 10% to be divided between the Minors). Status on close of estate is continued to April 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Berger that either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has to be filed by then.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-31-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laura Berger in Propria Persona.

Ms. Berger stated that she looked at form instructions, that she has identified all of the assets, that she knows there is a time period for filing of any claims and that she is not aware of any creditors to notify. Ms. Berger also stated that she knows the next step is to obtain an order and letters, that she needs to give copies of the letters to the three account (checking, money market account, investment account) holders and that she may need to convert them to Estate accounts once she determines interest rates. In addition, Ms. Berger stated that there is an auction house that sold some of her parents effects, that the auction house still has a painting that did not sell, that the painting is in Ohio, that her Mom has lived in an assisted living facility and that the house sold a couple of years ago as well as their effects. Furthermore, Md. Berger stated that the majority of her Mother's accounts were transfer on death accounts, that there is a small legacy (insurance) account, that when her Father passed away each of the Daughters received about $12,000.00 and that her Mother never cashed hers out. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). The will is admitted to probate. Bond is waived. Status on close of estate is set for February 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for December 27, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-14-2017 Continued To Aug-28-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laura Berger in Propria Persona.

The Court expressed concern about appointing an Executor that is not represented by an Attorney, stated that a person who is appointed is an Officer of the Court who is held to certain standards in performance, that the interest of other parties are affected, that many complicated issues need to be addressed when administering an estate, advised Ms. Berger that a Trust would need to be established and asked what the next step is. Ms. Berger stated that she is an Attorney, but she is not barred in California, that she is a member of the Florida Bar and that she practices for the Federal government. Ms. Berger also stated that she has no experience in probate, that she would like to hire an estate tax attorney, that she does not anticipate any other issues, that she now understands the issue of providing notice to everyone and that the next step is to provide notice to all of the asset holders. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 28, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Berger to look at the matter and come back and tell the Court what the next steps are.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
